Region with Most Avalanches

Most Common Weak layer

Facets

This dashboard aims to visualize different variables in facet layer avalanches. As seen in the map and plot below, avalanches by facet layer are the most common avalanche problem throughout the Utah forecast region. Data obtained through utahavalanchecenter.org.

Avalanches by Weak Layer Failure 2020-25

Average Depth of Facet layer

2.7 ft

Average Width of Avalanche by Facet

230 ft

Average Vertical Run of Avalanche by Facet

624 ft

Results
  • Overview

The plots shown in this dashboard provide an in depth understanding of where facet avalanches are most likely to occur in relation to aspect, as well as how the depth of the facet layer affects the size of the avalanche in both width and vertical run.

  • Discussion

I found that while southern aspects had the deepest average depth (3.1 ft), they had the least amount of facet released avalanches out of all aspects, while the majority (384) occurred on North Eastern facing slopes. This can be attributed to the difference in sun that each aspect receives. A southern slope receives the most sun out of any, which in the early season can melt early snowfall, reducing the risk of that early snow rotting until the next snowfall. A more northerly aspect does not receive as much sun, allowing a early snow-pack to sit and rot until normal season conditions persist.
